title: Dataset
description: Metadata and volume statistics for dense neuroAI datasets.

name.description: Full dataset name, as it should appear in citations and documentation.

short_name.description: Short display name used in figures and compact tables (ideally ≤ 12 characters).

reference.description: URL to the primary publication or official documentation page for this dataset.

subjects_n.description: Number of participants who contributed data to this dataset.

neuroimaging.description: Neural recording modalities. Each entry reports the total acquisition time in hours across all sessions and subjects.

fmri.description: BOLD fMRI acquisition time (hours), including all runs and sessions across the full dataset.

eeg.description: EEG acquisition time (hours), including all runs and sessions.

meg.description: MEG acquisition time (hours), including all runs and sessions.

ieeg.description: Intracranial EEG acquisition time (hours), including all runs and sessions.

tasks.description: Volume of relevant task dimensions. Each sub-field captures a distinct type of stimulus or behavioral engagement. Units vary by modality: images and contrasts are counts (#), all other stimulus and recording fields are hours (h).

video.description: Continuous video stimuli (hours). total_unique is the duration of distinct video content; *_with_repetition includes repeated viewings.

audio.description: Audio stimuli (hours), e.g. music or environmental sounds. Audio stimuli may include speech. total_unique is the duration of distinct audio content. *_with_repetition includes repeated listening.

speech_listening.description: Audio stimuli (hours) featuring spoken language, e.g. movies featuring dialogues, audiobooks, podcasts, or speech passages. The stimuli may have periods without speech, e.g. a pure action scene in a movie. total_unique is the duration of distinct audio content. *_with_repetition includes repeated listening.

text_reading.description: Text reading stimuli (hours), e.g. sentences or passages displayed on screen. total_unique is the duration of distinct reading content. *_with_repetition includes repeated reading of the same text.

resting_state.description: Resting-state scans (hours): no task instruction, no external stimulus. per_subject_unique is the amount of rest data recorded per subject in a single pass through the protocol.

game.description: Gameplay recordings (hours): active video-game sessions where participants produced motor responses. Captures the behavioral richness of naturalistic game interaction.

controlled.description: Volume of controlled experiments, in hours. Controlled experiments have a fixed trial structure designed to isolate specific cognitive or perceptual processes (e.g. task localizers, block designs). Use recording_hours rather than stimulus_modality because the relevant quantity is total scan time, not unique stimulus content.

contrasts.description: Number of canonical statistical contrasts that can be generated from the controlled experiments in this dataset (e.g. condition A vs. baseline, condition A vs. condition B). Each contrast yields one whole-brain statistical map per subject.

physiology.description: Peripheral physiological recording modalities (hours). Each entry reports total acquisition time; coverage may be less than neuroimaging if sensors were only deployed for a subset of sessions, or more if some recording sessions are available without neuroimaging.

respiration.description: Respiration belt recording (hours), measuring thoracic expansion during breathing.

plethysmograph.description: Photoplethysmograph / pulse oximeter recording (hours), capturing peripheral pulse waveform.

eda.description: Electrodermal activity (skin conductance) recording (hours), an index of sympathetic arousal.

eye_tracking.description: Eye tracking recording (hours), capturing gaze position and/or pupil diameter.

recording_hours.description: Total acquisition volume for a recording modality, expressed in hours.

total_h.description: Total hours pooled across all subjects and sessions.

stimulus_modality.description: Stimulus volume for a single modality, distinguishing unique content from repeated presentations.

total_unique.description: Total unique stimulus volume across all subjects (images: count of distinct files; all others: hours of distinct content). Each stimulus item is counted once regardless of how many times it was shown.

per_subject_unique.description: Unique stimulus volume per subject — the novel content each participant was exposed to, counted once per item.

total_with_repetition.description: Total stimulus volume including within-subject and across-subject repetitions (images: total presentations; all others: total hours of stimulus delivery).

per_subject_with_repetition.description: Stimulus volume per subject including within-session repetitions of the same item (images: average presentations per subject; all others: hours per subject).
